items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering a business license amnesty program and launching outreach efforts to shopping centers, likely to help previously unlicensed or non-compliant businesses get into compliance. This could affect local retail and small business operations across Cupertino shopping areas.

items.headline: New economic incentives announced for retail and startup businesses
items.summary: The city council approved economic development incentives aimed at attracting and supporting retail businesses, small businesses, and startups in Cupertino. This signals an active effort to revitalize local commercial activity and attract new business investment.

items.headline: Capitol Dental Office opens on North Capitol Avenue
items.summary: A $500,000 dental office is opening at 585 N Capitol Av, bringing a new healthcare service to the area. This is the only significant commercial project in this recent permit batch.

items.headline: City seeks input on federal community development grant funding
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission heard presentations from organizations applying for Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These federal funds support affordable housing and community development projects that directly benefit Sunnyvale residents.

items.headline: Palo Alto considers retail vitality policy changes to zoning code
items.summary: The Planning Commission is reviewing an ordinance to amend zoning and building regulations to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. This could affect where and how retail businesses operate in Palo Alto.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area undergoing land use and transportation study
items.summary: The Planning Commission is studying alternative land use and transportation scenarios for the San Antonio Road area. The study could lead to zoning changes or new development patterns in this region.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at 490 East Middlefield Road
items.summary: The City Council approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project on East Middlefield Road. This project will add new housing to Mountain View, which is relevant for South Bay residents tracking local development and housing supply.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program. If approved, this funding could support additional housing projects and policy changes that make it easier to build homes in Mountain View.
